title: Visual Proof of Cantor's Theorem
description: A diagram illustrating the diagonalization argument used to prove that no function from a set to its power set can be surjective, demonstrating that the power set has a strictly larger cardinality.
elements: Diagonal Set D = { s ∈ S | s ∉ f(s) }
Element d ∈ S (candidate for pre-image of D)
relationships: f maps each element of S to a subset in P(S)
D is constructed from the images of f
d cannot be the pre-image of D because if d ∈ f(d) then d ∉ D, and if d ∉ f(d) then d ∈ D, creating a contradiction
